summary_one_line: A parameter-entity reference whose name is immediately followed by ';' so xmlParsePEReference sees RAW == ';' and takes the false branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A DTD-context '%' parameter-entity reference (%name) terminated by a literal ';' character immediately after the parsed name, so that at parser.c:8015 the current char RAW equals ';'. The false branch requires the byte after the PE name to be exactly 0x3b (';').
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the false side 10/10 trials and its winning seeds carry a well-formed PE reference: winning seed 2 contains the byte run '[%.d;' ([ 25 ff 64 3b]) placing a ';' (0x3b) right after a '%'-introduced name, so RAW == ';' holds and the false branch (T=3 F=9 in W) is taken. The CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that get progressively closer to the byte the parser compares against (';'), letting vpc climb toward a terminated PEReference.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks the false side 8/10 trials; its blocking seeds reach line 8015 with the true side dominant (L: T=95 F=3), i.e. the byte following the parsed name is not ';'. The cmplog blocking seeds (e.g. '06 00 00 00 31 32 37 37 ... DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1') carry the DOCTYPE/SYSTEM skeleton but no '%name;' whose name is immediately followed by ';', so RAW != ';' stays true and the entity body at 8025+ is never reached.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The only technique delta between winner (value_profile_cmplog) and loser (cmplog) is value_profile: the CMP_MAP gradient buckets give partial-credit Hamming/prefix-distance feedback for the RAW vs ';' comparison region, so vpc retains intermediate seeds inching toward the exact ';' terminator. cmplog has the same I2S substitution as vpc but lacks the gradient feedback, so it cannot bank near-miss progress and gets stuck on the true (blocking) side.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of the RAW != ';' comparison in xmlParsePEReference.
The globally blocked side is the false branch and the winner is value_profile_cmplog over loser cmplog with delta value_profile.
value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch 10/10 while cmplog blocks it 8/10.
At the blocker line the winner takes the false (resolving) side while the loser is dominated by the true (blocking) side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L8015  T=3 F=9  T=95 F=3  if (RAW != ';') {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: A winning value_profile_cmplog seed contains a '%'-introduced reference followed by a ';' terminator byte.
Loser-blocking cmplog seeds carry the DOCTYPE/SYSTEM skeleton but no terminated %name; so they reach the blocker on the true side.
value_profile contributes a CMP_MAP gradient feedback signal that cmplog does not have.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's feedback is the same edge-bucket signal as naive, with no CMP_MAP gradient.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Feedback**: same edge-bucket signal as naive. The CMP buffer is
consumed by the mutator, not by feedback.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Not applicable / not required: the technique delta between winner (value_profile_cmplog) and loser (cmplog) is value_profile, so the differentiating mechanism is the CMP_MAP gradient, not I2SRandReplace (both fuzzers already carry I2S). The I2S-floor lineage check is only mandatory when claimed_mechanism is I2SRandReplace, so no db_query.py lineage call was run; the gradient claim rests on the MECHANISM CONTEXT difference (vpc adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets, cmplog feedback is edge-bucket only).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on RAW == ';' after a PE name where cmplog resolves the false branch as fast as value_profile_cmplog (no value_profile advantage), showing the gradient feedback is not what enables the winner.

weakest_evidence_point: Only one winning seed (seed 2) shows an explicit ';' terminator after a '%' reference; the other two winning seeds do not visibly contain a terminated %name; in the shown 64-byte window, so the byte-level dataflow link is partly inferential.
